name: Lysosomal surveillance response activation
summary: Lysosomal surveillance response activation is normalized for C. elegans healthspan evidence from partial v-ATPase perturbation.
mechanism_summary: Activates a lysosomal stress or surveillance response to test whether lysosome-mediated adaptation can improve organismal healthspan.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: C. elegans lysosomal surveillance response and healthspan study; Partial v-ATPase perturbation activated a lysosomal surveillance response and extended worm healthspan.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
